text: 1. A dialogue between two friends about city life and village life. Sabina		: How do you spend your days in Dhaka. Anika? Anika	: Not so good. I feel bored everywhere in this city? I always remember the days in my village. Sabina	: I think you can't cope with the city life. You can't adjust yourself with the new environment. Anika		: Yes, You are right. I think village life is easy and pleasing. Sabina	: But city life is a fast life. Nothing is easy here. You have to make everything easy for you. Anika	: You have to inhale polluted air. There is a frequent traffic jam. what a disgusting matter it is! Sabina	: Yes, There is some problems in city life. But you should realise that city offers you every necessary things. You can't get all the advantages in villages. Anika	: But we have fresh air, open field to play and the beautiful..... cornfields spreading to the horizon. Sabina	: Absolutely right! But think the other side of village life. you have no modern medical facilities and standard educational institution in the villages. Anika	: Yes, the villagers are deprived of modern amenities of life but the villagers live peacefully maintaining relationship with the people from all communities. Sabina	: Yes, there exists a peaceful atmosphere and co-operation among the villagers. It's a good sign. Anika	: We hardly see such type of co-existence in city life. The inhabitants of the cities are self-centered and remain busy with them themselves. I can't like it. Sabina		: You quite right! Anika		: Let's come to the point and do something for the villagers. Sabina		: Ok, Let's do that.

text: 3. A dialogue between me and my friend about the importance of tree plantation. Myself	: How's it going, Sumi? Sumi		: I'm very fine and you? Myself	: I'm ok. But I'm anxious about our environment as it is polluting in an alarming way. I'm thinking how I can prevent it. Sumi		: Oh, how fast our environment deteriorate! It is a matter of great concern. We should address the issue as early as we can. Myself	: You're right. But how can we address the issue? What should we do? It is not an individual act. We have to think of it with care and find a quick solution. otherwise we have to face a great problem in future. Sumi	: I think tree plantation programme can play the key role to stop environmental  pollution. Myself	: Yes! you're quite right. Tree are the important elements that can save our environment. Our class teacher said trees produce huge amount of oxygen and absorb carbon dioxide which put the environment in a state of balance. Sumi	: Yes, we should plant more trees in our home stead to ensure a good atmosphere. I  know the benefit of trees. The trees help us and keep the environment clean and pure. Myself	: But we have to work regarding the issue collectively. Only you and I cannot make it successful. So we have to carry out a tree plantation campaign to keep our  environment free from all kinds of pollution. Sumi		: Yes, You're right. Trees are not only beneficial to environment but also they provide us with fruits, fire-wood, furniture and raw- materials of industries. Myself	: Right! Trees are of great use and benefit. So we should plant two trees, in case we cut one tree. Sumi		: Thank you. Let's move to make the mission a success.

text: 4. A dialogue between me and my friend about necessity of learning English. Myself		: Hello! Anika. How are you? What's about your short course in English? Anika		: I'm fine. I always go to the town Hall English language club to Practise speaking English. Myself	: Fine! you are on a good mission. We should learn English to challenge with the present needs of the challenging world. As English is used as an international language, We can't ignore the necessity of learning English. Anika		: That's why I'm learning English. Look! It's an age of globalization where everything is done by English. We cannot keep pace with the modern way of life without knowing English. Myself		: Yes, You're totally right. We see that internet, facebook, Skype and other social communication systems are being operated in English. If we are out of English, you will be out the world. Anika	: Certainly! if we talk about learning education in higher level at home and abroad, we cannot set English aside. Myself	: You know that the famous books at every level of education are written in English. More over, the books which are written in other languages of the world, have been translated into English so that the people skilled in English can apprehend the books. Anika	: That's Why we shouldn't keep our self back in learning English. More over, we should urge the students to learn English. Myself	: Yes, if they aren't aware of English, they will not get a good job as good jobs need good English knowing persons. Anika		; Let's move for the mission.

text: 5. Dialogue: Importance of Reading Newspapers Kamal		: Hello Jamal! What are you doing so early in the morning? Jamal		: Hi Kamal! I am reading today’s newspaper. It has become my daily habit. Kamal		: That’s good. But tell me, why do you think reading newspapers is so important? Jmal		: Well, newspapers are called the mirror of the world. They provide us with news and views about what is happening around us and across the globe. Without newspapers, we would remain ignorant of current affairs. Kamal		: That’s very true. A student who reads newspapers regularly gains knowledge of history, politics, economics, culture, science, and technology. It helps to become smart and well-informed. Jamal		: Exactly. Newspapers also play a vital role in education. For instance, students can find reports and features that enrich their essays, debates, and general knowledge. It also helps improve vocabulary and language skills. Kamal		: Yes, I agree. I remember last week I used information from a newspaper article for my school assignment. It impressed my teacher a lot. Jamal		: Another great benefit is that newspapers make us conscious citizens. They create awareness about social problems like corruption, drug abuse, deforestation, and illiteracy. By reading them, we learn how to take responsibility. Kamal		: Absolutely. They also keep us updated about government decisions, economic conditions, and international relations. In this way, newspapers connect us with the entire world. Jamal		: That’s right. And you know, newspapers also provide recreation. They have sports pages, comics, puzzles, and features on culture and literature. So, they are a source of both knowledge and entertainment. Kamal		: Very true! For job seekers, newspapers are also useful because they publish job circulars, exam notices, and important advertisements. Many young people depend on newspapers to build their career. Jamal		: Yes. Moreover, newspapers shape public opinion. They highlight the problems of the people and put pressure on authorities to take action. In this way, newspapers are considered the “fourth estate” of democracy. Kamal		: You are absolutely right. So, newspapers are not only a source of information but also a powerful instrument for social change. Jamal		: Well said, Kamal. That is why every educated person should cultivate the habit of reading newspapers daily. Kamal		: I agree with you completely. From today, I will make it a routine to read the newspaper every morning. Jamal		: That’s great! Let’s encourage our friends too. Reading newspapers will surely help us to become enlightened citizens. Kamal		: Yes, Jamal. A nation can prosper only if its people are well-informed, and newspapers play the most vital role in that.

text: 6. A dialogue between me and Anika on visiting a book fair. Myself		: Hi, Anika! what about you? I went to your house, but couldn't find you. Anika		: Oh, really? I'm sorry for not finding me at my residence. Myself		: It's ok. But where did you go? Anika		: I visited the Ekushe Boi Mela with my father yesterday. I had a very exciting moment at the Bangla Academy. Myself		: Absolutely right you are! Visiting a book fair is always interesting and exciting. More over, it is a Ekushe book Fair. Anika		: Certainly! Ekushe Bio Mela is the largest book-fair in our country. The fair is organized by Bangla Academy in commemoration of the language martyrs who laid down their lives for the cause of our mother tongue. Myself		: Definitely! Ekushe Boi Mela draws the attention of all kinds of readers and book lovers. It's a month long book fair that is participated by the famous publisters of the country. Anika		: That's why I couldn't miss the dortunity. Myself		: How could you enjoy the fair? What's about the visitors of the fair? Anika		: Oh, I had a wonderful moment. There were as many as two lac visitors yesterday. Myself		: How many books did you buy and what were those on? Anika		: I bought ten books. The books are on the war of liberation, science fiction, novels and poems. Myself		: I want to go to the fair tomorrow. Could you please accompany me? Anika		: Ok, no problem. Which books do you want to buy? Myself		: I'm always interested in the newly published books. More over, I'm weak at the famous writers' books like Rabindranath Tagore, Kazi Nazrul Islam, Jasim Uddin and the contemporary writers of our country. Anika		: Your choice is very fine. I appreciate you that you are going to visit the book fair. It's your right decision. Myself	: Thank you that you are agree to go with me. Certainly, we will have a fine day tomorrow at the Bangla Academy. Anika		: Ok, Let's move.

text: 7. A dialogue between two friends about plans and programs after examination. Myself		: What is about your plan after JSC examination, Raka? Sabina	: Thank you for your query about my plan and program. The examination is about to end and I am thinking of my plan. But I have not yet finalized the plans. Myself	: I have reached a decision about what I should do after the examination.Everything is going smoothly on my part. Sabina		: what is the plan, Sabina? Myself	: Oh, you know that every year I go to my village home to spend the vacation with my relatives and friends there. Sabina		: Is there any special plan at your village home? Myself	: Of course. I cannot sit idle there. Every moment I remain busy with illiterate people of my village. They are so eager to be literate! Sabina	: Oh! It's a great plan. We all should go with such kind of plan to educate the people to make the country a literate one. Myself		: I have also planned for another program. Sabina		: What is it? Myself	: I have decided to make a tour to kuakata sea beach. I will go there with my family members. My father will be on leave at that time. Sabina	: I have also such plan. I always like to pay a visit to an interesting place. Kuakata is a wonderful place. Myself	: Wonderful! I invite you to go with me. My tour will certainly get a new dimension to have you with me. Sabina		: Ok! Let's make the plan a success.

text: 8. A dialogue about benefit of early rising/ morning walk. Sunayna	: Good morning, Sabina! How are you? Anika		: I'm fine and you? Sunayna	: I'm fine too. You look so fresh and energetic today. text: 13. On a dailouge on frequent road accidents and it’s causes remidies. Asif	: Hello! How're you? Labib	: I'm fine. What about you? Asif	: Not well. One of my neighbours died last night in a tragic road accident. Labib	: Very sad! To speak the truth, nowadays it has become a common incident in our country. Asif	: Yes, it's really a matter of serious concern nowadays but our policy makers seem indifferent to the fact. Labib	: Absolutely true. But can you tell me the causes of frequent road accidents across the country? Asif	: The causes are manifold. Reckless driving, lack of skilled traffic police, ignorance of the drivers tendency of overtaking, violation of traffic signals and rules, etc. contribute greatly to the frequent road accidents. Labib	: You are right. The increasing number of vehicles is also responsible for road accident. With more vehicles, the chances of accidents also rise. Asif	: Exactly. The traffic in our city is terrible. People don't follow the traffic rules properly. Overspeed is also a major cause of accidents. Labib	: True. People drive way too fast, even in busy areas or on roads. Asif	: You said very well, Labib. Overspeeding is one of the leading causes of accidents. When drivers drive in overspeed, accidents take place with sharp turns. Labib	: You are absolutely right. The conditions of roads are extremely poor. These are also causing many road accidents. Asif	: Many people drive under the influence of alcohol or drugs, which can easily lead to a fatal crash. Labib	: That's one of the most serious issues. It’s not just dangerous for the person driving, but for everyone on the road. Asif	: Have you noticed people using their phones while driving? It is another cause of road accidents. Labib	: Yes, distracted driving has become one of the leading causes of accidents nowadays. Right! Immediate steps should be taken to reduce road accidents. What do you think? Asif	: Yes, of course. Strict laws should be introduced in this regard. Illiterate and careless drivers should be identified, and their driving licenses should be cancelled. Labib	: Certainly! Besides, drivers should be given proper training, and the Roads and Highways Department should be more cautious in this regard. Asif	: I agree with you. Also, the government should focus on improving road infrastructure. Labib	: Definitely. And people should also be encouraged to use public transportation. It reduces traffic congestion and ultimately accidents. Asif	: Absolutely! People should also be made aware of traffic rules and signals. In this way, we can minimize road accidents. Labib	: You're right! We all have to come forward to raise public awareness in this concern. Exactly! Asif	: Thank you very much for such a nice discussion. Labib	: You're most welcome. See you again.

As a student you should have a Library card to borrow  a book. Now, you take this form and  fill it up. Myself 	: OK! Am I required to deposit a membership fee? Librarian 	: Yes . you have to deposite TK 50 in cash. Myself 	:   What's  facilities  I will be awarded? Librarian 	: Oh,you can enjoy so many  facilities from this library. you  can visit the library and be allowed to stay here for reading books till the  opening time.  you can  also borrow books, if you wish. Myself 	: That's great! Library time please? Librarian 	: The library remains open every day from  10am to 5 pm for the readers. Myself 	: How many books do you have in your your library and what type of books are in your collection? Librarian 	: We have a huge collection of books. These are based on academic,story, novel, Liberation war and so on! We also have newspapers and magazines. Myself 	: Wow ! That's fine! I How many books hure can I borrow  from the library and for how long? Librarian 	: Yes!  you can borrow two books at a time for two  weeks. Myself 	: Thank you for the information. I have already filled the form up and deposited the required fee for the card. Take the form, please.! Librarian 	: Yes, of course. Thank you so much. Please take your card. text: 15. A Dialogue Between two  friends  About the Importance of Physical Exercise Rahim		: Hello Karim! How are you doing today? Karim		: Hi Rahim! I’m doing great. What about you? Rahim		: I’m fine too. But lately, I’ve been feeling a bit tired and lazy. I think I need to change something in my daily routine. Karim		: Oh, really? Have you been doing any physical exercise lately? Rahim		: Not really. I mostly wake up late and go straight to studying or using my phone. I hardly move around. Karim		: That might be the reason. Physical exercise is really important for a healthy life. It helps you stay active and energetic. Rahim		: Yes, I’ve heard that before. But I never paid much attention. Can you tell me more about the benefits? Karim		: Sure! Regular physical exercise improves our blood circulation, strengthens muscles, and increases our stamina. It also helps in keeping our heart and lungs healthy. Rahim		: That sounds great. But does it help with studies too? Karim	 	: Of course! Exercise keeps the mind fresh and improves concentration. After exercising, I feel more focused and motivated to study. Rahim		: Wow, I really need that! I often feel sleepy and distracted while studying. Karim		: Then you should definitely start exercising. Even a 30-minute walk in the morning or some free-hand exercises at home can make a big difference. Rahim		: But I don’t know where to start. Can you help me? Karim		: Absolutely! I go for a morning walk in the park near our house. After that, I do some stretching and basic workouts. You can join me tomorrow if you like. Rahim		: That would be great! I think exercising with a friend will also keep me motivated. Karim		: Definitely. It’s always more fun when you have a partner. We can also set some goals and track our progress together. Rahim		: That’s a good idea. By the way, is physical exercise good for mental health too? Karim		: Yes, it is. Exercise reduces stress, anxiety, and even depression. It makes you feel happy and confident. That’s why doctors and teachers also encourage it. Rahim		: I never realized it has so many benefits. Thanks for explaining everything so clearly. Karim		: You’re welcome, Rahim. I’m glad you’re taking interest in your health. Let’s meet tomorrow at 6 a.m. in the park. Rahim		: Sure! I’ll set my alarm today and get ready. See you tomorrow! Karim		: See you! And don’t forget your water bottle!

text: 16. A dialogue on the importance of games and sports Nabin		: Hi Rahul! I saw you playing cricket after school today. Don’t you think you should be spending more time on your studies, especially with exams coming up? Rahul		: Hi Nabin! I do study regularly, but I also believe that playing games is equally important. A healthy body helps us focus better on studies. Nabin		: But aren’t games just for fun? I always thought that if we want to do well in life, we should spend our free time reading or revising. Rahul		: That’s a common thought, but actually, sports play a big role in our overall development. When we play, our body stays fit, and our mind feels relaxed. This makes it easier to study with more concentration. Nabin		: Hmm, I didn’t think about it that way. But what if someone gets injured or tired after playing? Rahul		: Injuries can happen anywhere, even at home. But when we play under proper rules and with care, the risk is very low. And feeling a little tired after playing is actually good—it helps you sleep better and wake up fresh the next day. Nabin		: That sounds reasonable. But how do sports help us in the long term? Rahul		: Great question! Games and sports teach us discipline, patience, leadership, and teamwork. These are life skills we need in every career. Many successful people say that playing sports helped them build confidence and manage stress. Nabin		: I never thought games could teach us so much. I always focused only on exams and marks. Rahul		: Marks are important, of course. But physical fitness, mental health, and strong values are equally important. That’s why schools have playgrounds and sports periods too. Nabin		: You’ve really changed my view, Rahul. I think I should take some time out for sports too. Rahul		: Absolutely, Nabin! text: 17. Dialogue: Importance of Mobile Phone for Learning Rafiq		: Good morning, Junaid. How are your studies going? Junaid	: Good morning, Rafiq. Alhamdulillah, they’re going well. text: 18. A dialogue on favourite hobby Sabina		: Hi Anika! How are you doing today? Anika		: I’m doing well, Sabina. How about you? Sabina		: I’m good too. I was just reading a book on hobbies. It made me curious — do you have a favourite hobby? Anika		: Yes, I do! My favourite hobby is reading books. I love spending my free time with books. Sabina		: That’s really nice. What kind of books do you like to read? Anika		: I mostly enjoy storybooks, adventure novels, and sometimes biographies. Reading helps me learn new words and ideas. What’s your hobby? Sabina		: My favourite hobby is gardening. I’ve been taking care of a small garden at home for the past two years. Anika		: Wow! That sounds very peaceful. What do you grow in your garden? Sabina		: I grow many kinds of flowers like roses, marigolds, and sunflowers. I also grow vegetables like tomatoes, chillies, and spinach. Anika		: That’s amazing! Gardening must take a lot of patience and care. Sabina		: Yes, it does. I water the plants every day, remove weeds, and sometimes add fertilizer. It feels great to see the plants grow and bloom. Anika		: I can imagine! Gardening is a very healthy hobby too. You get fresh air and stay active. Sabina		: Exactly! It also teaches me responsibility and helps me stay close to nature. Anika		: Do you do it alone or does your family help you? Sabina		: Sometimes my younger sister helps me. My parents also encourage me a lot. They say it’s a good use of my free time. Anika		: That’s really nice, Sabina. I think I should visit your garden sometime. I would love to see the flowers and vegetables. Sabina		: Sure! You can come over this weekend. Let’s always give time to our hobbies besides our studies. Anika		: Absolutely! Alright then, see you soon! Sabina		: See you, Anika!

text: 20. A dialogue about the importance of internet. Riya		: Good morning, Arman! How are you? Arman	: Good morning, Riya! I’m fine, thank you. What about you? Riya		: I’m doing well. I was just thinking about how dependent we’ve become on the internet nowadays. What’s your opinion about it? Arman	: That’s a very relevant topic. In my opinion, the internet has become an essential part of our lives. From education to communication, it is involved in almost every aspect of modern life. Riya		: Yes, I agree. During the pandemic, we continued our education only because of the internet. Online classes, exams, assignments — everything was done virtually. Arman	: Exactly. The internet has made education much more flexible. We can attend classes from home, watch video lectures, and even take online courses from foreign universities. Riya		: True. It also helps me with research. Whenever I have doubts or need more information, I search online. It saves time and effort. Arman	: That’s right. Search engines like Google, educational platforms like Khan Academy or YouTube, and digital libraries have made knowledge more accessible than ever before. Riya		: Apart from education, I also use the internet to stay in touch with my cousins who live abroad. We chat regularly and even do video calls. Arman	: Communication has indeed become easier and faster. Social media platforms, emails, and instant messaging apps have brought people closer, no matter where they are in the world. Riya		: The internet also supports online banking and shopping. My parents often pay bills and buy things online, which saves both time and energy. Arman	: Absolutely. Many businesses now operate fully online. The internet has opened new opportunities in freelancing, remote work, digital marketing, and more. Riya		: However, isn’t there a dark side too? Like cyberbullying, data theft, or wasting time on social media? Arman	: Yes, the internet has both advantages and disadvantages. It depends on how we use it. If we are careful and responsible, we can avoid the negative sides. Using strong passwords, not sharing personal information, and spending time wisely are very important. Riya		: That makes sense. So, the internet is like a double-edged sword — useful but dangerous if misused. Arman	: Exactly. We should use it as a tool for learning, growing, and connecting with others - not as a source of distraction or harm. Riya		: Thanks, Arman. Talking to you really helped me understand the importance of using the internet wisely. Arman	: My pleasure, Riya. It’s always good to discuss such meaningful topic.

text: 21. A dialogue  on how to eradicate  illiteracy  problem  of Bangladesh. Alim		: Assalamu Alaikum, Jasim! How are you today? Jasim		: Wa Alaikum Salam, Alim! I’m good, Alhamdulillah. But I was thinking about something serious yesterday—our country’s literacy problem. It’s really worrying. Alim		: You are right. Bangladesh has made progress in education, but illiteracy is still a big challenge. Many people, especially in rural areas, cannot read or write. Jasim		: Exactly. I think one of the main reasons is poverty. Many children cannot afford to go to school. They have to work to support their families. Alim		: That’s true. Poverty forces parents to prioritize earning over education. To tackle illiteracy, the government should provide free education and stipends to poor families. This way, children won’t have to drop out. Jasim		: Yes, financial support can help. But I think another problem is the lack of awareness. Some parents don’t understand the importance of education, especially for girls. Alim		: That’s a big issue too. Communities need awareness programs to encourage parents to send all their children to school. Local leaders and teachers can play a vital role in spreading this message. Jasim		: Also, our schools need better facilities. Many schools in villages don’t have proper classrooms, toilets, or libraries. Students don’t feel motivated to study in such conditions. Alim		: You are absolutely right. Improving school infrastructure is essential. Moreover, we need more trained and committed teachers who can teach creatively and make learning interesting. Jasim		: Another solution is adult education. Many grown-ups missed their chance to go to school. If we provide evening or weekend classes, they can learn basic reading and writing. Alim		: That’s a great idea. Literacy campaigns for adults can have a huge impact. Educated adults can also encourage their children to study. Jasim		: And we should use technology wisely. Online classes, mobile learning apps, and radio programs can reach remote areas. Alim		: True. Technology can overcome distance and shortage of teachers. But the government and NGOs must work together to ensure every person has access. Jasim		: So, in short, we need financial support for poor families, awareness programs, better schools, trained teachers, adult education, and the use of technology. If we focus on these, I believe illiteracy can be significantly reduced. Alim		: Absolutely, Jasim. It will take time, but with determination and proper planning, Bangladesh can become an educated nation. Jasim		: Yes, let’s hope for that day soon!

text: 22. A dialogue one causes and effects of water pollution. Alim		: Hello, Jasim! Have you noticed how polluted the river near our village has become recently? Jasim	: Yes, Alim. It’s really alarming. I think water pollution is becoming a serious problem everywhere, not just here. Alim		: Absolutely. There are many causes behind it. One major cause is industrial waste. Factories often dump harmful chemicals directly into rivers and lakes. Jasim		: That’s true. Besides industrial waste, domestic waste also plays a big role. People throw garbage, plastic, and even leftover food into water bodies without realizing the harm it causes. Alim		: Another cause is agricultural runoff. Farmers use pesticides and fertilizers, which eventually get washed into rivers and ponds during rainfall. These chemicals contaminate the water. Jasim		: I’ve also read that oil spills and sewage disposal are important causes. Oil spills from boats and ships pollute large areas of water, and untreated sewage introduces harmful bacteria and viruses. Alim		: Exactly. These causes not only damage water quality but also affect aquatic life. Fish, plants, and other creatures cannot survive in polluted water. Jasim		: And it affects humans too. Contaminated water can cause diseases like cholera, diarrhea, and hepatitis. Drinking polluted water is very dangerous, especially for children. Alim		: Yes, and water pollution also affects agriculture. If polluted water is used for irrigation, it can destroy crops and make the soil infertile over time. Jasim		: Not to mention the impact on the environment. Water bodies lose their natural beauty, and ecosystems become unbalanced. Many animals die, and some species even face extinction. Alim		: That’s why we must take action. Public awareness is essential. People should be educated about the harmful effects of water pollution and encouraged to keep water bodies clean. Jasim		: I agree. Governments should also enforce strict laws against dumping industrial and domestic waste into rivers and lakes. Alim		: Moreover, promoting the use of eco-friendly products, proper waste management, and recycling can significantly reduce pollution. Jasim		: Absolutely. Small steps by everyone can make a big difference. Clean water is essential for life, and it is our responsibility to protect it. Alim		: You’re right, Jasim. If we act now, future generations can enjoy pure and safe water. Jasim		: Let’s start by spreading awareness in our community. Together, we can fight water pollution.

Jasim		: Not to mention the impact on the environment. Water bodies lose their natural beauty, and ecosystems become unbalanced. Many animals die, and some species even face extinction. Alim		: That’s why we must take action. Public awareness is essential. People should be educated about the harmful effects of water pollution and encouraged to keep water bodies clean. Jasim		: I agree. Governments should also enforce strict laws against dumping industrial and domestic waste into rivers and lakes. Alim		: Moreover, promoting the use of eco-friendly products, proper waste management, and recycling can significantly reduce pollution. Jasim		: Absolutely. Small steps by everyone can make a big difference. Clean water is essential for life, and it is our responsibility to protect it. Alim		: You’re right, Jasim. If we act now, future generations can enjoy pure and safe water. Jasim		: Let’s start by spreading awareness in our community. Together, we can fight water pollution.

text: 23. Dialogue: The Bad Effects of Deforestation Kamal		: Hello Jamal! You look a bit thoughtful today. What are you thinking about? Jamal		: Hi Kamal! Actually, I was reading an article about deforestation and its harmful impacts. It made me really worried. Kamal		: Yes, you are right. Deforestation is one of the most alarming problems in our country and also in the whole world. Do you know why it is so serious? Jamal		: Of course. Trees are called the lungs of nature. If we cut them recklessly, we are destroying the balance of our environment. For example, deforestation causes global warming. Kamal		: Exactly. Trees absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen. If the number of trees decreases, the amount of carbon dioxide increases. As a result, the earth’s temperature rises, and climate change becomes more severe. Jamal		: That’s true. Another bad effect is soil erosion. When trees are cut down, the soil becomes loose. Heavy rain easily washes away the topsoil, which is very important for agriculture. Kamal		: You are absolutely right. This ultimately reduces the fertility of land and harms crop production. Thus, our food supply also gets threatened. Jamal		: Yes, and deforestation also destroys the habitats of wild animals. Many species of birds and animals lose their shelter and food. Some even become extinct. Kamal		: That is very sad. I read that the Royal Bengal Tiger in our Sundarbans is under threat mainly because of the destruction of forest areas. Jamal		: Exactly. Not only that, deforestation causes floods and droughts. When there are no trees to hold rainwater, rivers overflow and cause floods. On the other hand, in dry seasons, lack of trees leads to drought. Kamal		: Hmm, and floods and droughts are common in Bangladesh. If we don’t protect our forests, these natural disasters will increase more and more. Jamal		: Another serious effect is desertification. Places that once were green and fertile may turn into barren deserts if deforestation continues. Kamal		: You are right. And we should not forget that forests provide us with timber, fruits, herbs, and raw materials for many industries. If deforestation goes on, our economy will also suffer. Jamal		: Very true. So, I think awareness is the first step. People should know about the terrible consequences of deforestation. Kamal		: Yes, and the government should also take strict steps to stop illegal logging. At the same time, we must plant more trees to replace the ones that are cut down. Jamal		: That’s the only solution. If we want to survive, we must protect our forests and make our environment green again. Kamal		: Absolutely. Let us take a vow that we will never cut trees unnecessarily and will always encourage others to plant more trees. Jamal		: Agreed! Together we can save nature and make a better future.

text: 25.  Dialogue: Merits and Demerits of Using Mobile Phones Anika		: Hello Hira! You look quite busy with your mobile phone. What’s going on? Hira		: Hi Anika! I was just checking some messages and reading today’s news. You know, mobile phones have made life so easy. Anika		: Yes, I agree. One of the greatest merits is that they connect us instantly. We can talk to friends and family anywhere in the world within seconds. Hira		: That’s right! Communication has become cheaper and faster. Even video calls help us feel closer to our loved ones. Anika		: Another big advantage is education. Mobile phones allow students to attend online classes, watch tutorials, and download study materials. Hira		: Exactly! During the pandemic, mobiles became the main tool for education. They are like small classrooms in our hands. Anika		: True, but don’t you think mobiles also have some demerits? Many students waste time playing games instead of studying. Hira		: That’s a good point. Overuse of mobile phones can lead to addiction. Many young people spend hours scrolling social media. Anika		: Yes, and that addiction harms their studies, health, and even their personal life. Eye problems, headaches, and lack of sleep are common now. Hira		: Absolutely! But we cannot ignore the positive sides either. For example, mobiles are helpful in emergencies. One call can save lives. Anika		: Very true. GPS is also a great merit. While traveling, mobiles guide us and prevent us from losing our way. Hira		: Right! Mobile phones also support business. People can buy, sell, or even work online from home. That has created new opportunities. Anika		: But on the other hand, mobiles sometimes spoil real-life communication. People spend more time with their phones than with their families. Hira		: That’s sad but true. Social media can also spread false news or harmful content. If used blindly, it misguides people. Anika		: Another demerit is cybercrime. Hackers can steal personal data through mobile phones. Many people suffer financial loss. Hira		: Yes, that’s a serious issue. Moreover, excessive use of mobiles reduces physical activities. Many people prefer sitting at home with their phones instead of playing outside. Anika		: Exactly. That’s why balance is very important. We must use mobile phones for learning, communication, and safety, but avoid misuse. Hira		: Well said, Anika. Mobile phones are like a double-edged sword—if used properly, they are a blessing; if misused, they become a curse. Anika		: I completely agree. Let’s promise to use them wisely and encourage others to do the same. Hira		: Yes, that’s the best way to enjoy the merits and avoid the demerits. Anika		: I feel much better talking with you. Hira		: Thanks a lot.

text: 26. Dialogue: The Bad Effects of Smoking Anika		: Hello Hira! You look very upset today. What happened? Hira		: Hi Anika! I just visited my uncle at the hospital. He has been suffering from lung problems because of smoking. It really made me sad. Anika		: Oh, that’s unfortunate. Smoking is indeed very harmful. It destroys health slowly and silently. Hira		: Yes, doctors say smoking damages the lungs, causes cancer, and weakens the heart. But still many people continue this bad habit. Anika		: You’re right. Smoking is not just harmful to the smoker but also to the people around. Passive smokers, especially children, suffer a lot when they inhale the smoke. Hira		: Exactly. I read that tobacco contains nicotine, which is addictive. Once people start smoking, it becomes very hard for them to stop. Anika		: That’s one of the worst effects. Addiction not only ruins health but also wastes money. Many people spend a large part of their income on cigarettes. Hira		: True. Poor families suffer most because instead of buying food or medicine, smokers waste money on smoking. Anika		: Another bad effect is on the environment. Cigarette butts pollute the streets, and smoke pollutes the air we breathe. Hira		: Yes, and smoking also reduces a person’s lifespan. Smokers often look older, weaker, and less energetic than non-smokers. Anika		: Very true. It also creates social problems. Many schools and offices forbid smoking, but some people still do it secretly, setting a bad example for others. Hira		: That’s right. Teenagers often start smoking just to look smart or stylish, but later they regret it when their health gets damaged. Anika		: I completely agree. Smoking also causes bad breath, stained teeth, and an unpleasant smell, which makes people socially uncomfortable. Hira		: Yes, Anika. My uncle said he wishes he had never started smoking. Now he is suffering and spending a lot of money on treatment. Anika		: That is so sad. If people were more aware, they would avoid this deadly habit. Governments also warn people by printing “Smoking is injurious to health” on cigarette packets. Hira		: Unfortunately, many ignore these warnings. They think smoking gives them relaxation, but in reality, it slowly kills them. Anika		: Absolutely! Life is precious, and health is wealth. Smoking steals both. It is better never to start, because prevention is easier than cure. Hira		: Well said. Let us always say no to smoking and encourage others to quit this dangerous habit. Anika		: Agreed, Hira. Together we can spread awareness and save lives.

text: 27. Dialogue  on  Advantages and Disadvantages of rural life and urban life. Tabassum	: Hello Faria! How are you today? Faria		: I’m fine, Tabassum. What about you? Tabassum	: I’m good too. I was just thinking about the differences between village life and city life. Do you prefer living in a village or in a city? Faria		: That’s an interesting question. Honestly, I feel village life has many advantages. It is calm, quiet, and free from pollution. The green fields, fresh air, and simple lifestyle make it very peaceful. Tabassum	: You’re absolutely right. In villages, people can enjoy the beauty of nature. They get fresh vegetables, fruits, milk, and fish. Life there is simple and less stressful compared to the city. Faria		: Yes, people in villages are cooperative, friendly, and helpful. They live close to each other, share joys and sorrows, and maintain strong social bonds. Don’t you think that’s a big advantage? Tabassum	: Of course. But village life also has some disadvantages. There are not enough schools, colleges, or universities in rural areas. So students often have to go to towns or cities for higher education. Faria		: That’s true. Besides, villages lack modern hospitals, doctors, and medical facilities. In times of emergency, it becomes really difficult for villagers to get proper treatment. Tabassum	: Exactly. On the other hand, city life is full of facilities. In cities, you can find good educational institutions, modern hospitals, and plenty of job opportunities. Faria		: Yes, cities also have better communication, electricity, transportation, and entertainment. People can enjoy theatres, shopping malls, restaurants, and cultural programs. Tabassum	: That’s right. But city life has its disadvantages too. The roads are always crowded with vehicles, and traffic jams waste a lot of valuable time. Faria		: Not only that, but the air and water in cities are polluted. Factories, smoke, garbage, and noise make life unhealthy. People often suffer from stress, tension, and various diseases. Tabassum	: Another problem is that life in cities is too fast and competitive. People are always busy with their own work. They hardly know their neighbors and rarely have time for social bonding. Faria		: True. That’s why many people call city life mechanical. In contrast, villages may not have all the facilities, but they have peace, honesty, and unity. Tabassum	: Well said, Faria. So I think both village and city life have advantages and disadvantages. Village life is peaceful but lacks facilities, while city life is comfortable but full of problems. Faria		: I agree. If we could combine the good sides of both, life would be really perfect. Tabassum	: Absolutely. Let’s hope for a balanced lifestyle in the future. Faria		: Yes, that would be wonderful. Dialogue  on –

text: 28. The bad effects of adopting unfair means in the exam Myself	: Hello Asfia! You look worried today. Is everything okay? Asfia		: Hi! I’m a bit stressed. Our exams are coming soon, and I keep thinking about all the pressure. Myself	: I understand. Exams are always stressful. But we should face them honestly. Asfia		: I know, but sometimes I wonder if using unfair means could help me get better marks. Myself	: Oh Asfia! That’s not a good idea at all. Adopting unfair means in exams can have many bad effects. Asfia		: Really? I think if I cheat just once, no one will know, and I can score well. Myself	: That may be true for a moment, but in the long run, it is very harmful. First of all, it destroys your honesty and integrity. Once you start cheating, it becomes a habit. Asfia		: I never thought about it like that. Myself	: Yes, and cheating prevents you from actually learning. If you use unfair means, you may get good marks, but you won’t gain real knowledge. That can affect your future education and career. Asfia		: That’s true. I guess I won’t understand the lessons properly if I rely on unfair means. Myself	: Exactly. Moreover, if you are caught cheating, the consequences can be very serious. You may fail the exam, be punished by the school, or even be banned from exams. It can ruin your reputation forever. Asfia		: Oh, I didn’t realize that. I thought it was just a small thing. Myself	: Small things can become big problems. Also, cheating can make you feel guilty and anxious. Constant fear of being caught increases stress and affects mental health. Asfia		: I see. So, it’s really not worth it. Myself	: Absolutely. Instead of adopting unfair means, we should focus on hard work, regular study, and time management. Success achieved honestly gives true confidence and satisfaction. Asfia		: You are right. Hard work is the real key to success. I should start preparing honestly from today. Myself	: That’s the spirit! Remember, exams are not only about marks; they are about learning and improving yourself. Cheating may give temporary success, but honesty always brings long-term benefits. Asfia		: Thank you for guiding me. I will never try unfair means again. I’ll work hard and trust my abilities. Myself	: Good decision, Asfia. Believe in yourself, and you will do well!

text: 29. Dialogue on Early  Rising and disadvantages of Late  Rising. Tabassum	: Good morning, Faria! You look fresh today. Did you wake up early? Faria		: Good morning, Tabassum! Yes, I woke up early today. I believe early rising has many benefits. Tabassum	: Absolutely! Waking up early makes you feel active and energetic throughout the day. You can complete your tasks on time and stay healthy. Faria		: Yes, I also think early risers enjoy the quiet morning hours. It’s peaceful, and they can study, meditate, or plan their day properly. Tabassum	: That’s true. Early rising also improves concentration and discipline. People who wake up early tend to be more productive and successful in life. Faria		: I agree. Fresh air in the morning is good for health, and exercising in the morning keeps the body fit. Early risers also avoid the stress of rushing in the morning. Tabassum	: On the other hand, some people prefer late rising. They sleep late at night and wake up later in the morning. Faria		: Yes, and late rising can have its advantages too. Some people feel more creative and active during the evening and night. Their mind works better when they follow their natural body rhythm. Tabassum	: That’s interesting. Late risers can stay up late to study, work, or complete creative tasks without disturbance. Some writers, artists, and thinkers prefer night hours for concentration. Faria		: Exactly! Late rising allows flexibility. People who wake up late can enjoy longer rest and sleep properly, which is also essential for health. Tabassum	: True, but late risers may lose the quiet and fresh morning hours, and their day may feel shorter. They sometimes miss morning sunlight, which is good for the body. Faria		: That’s correct. So, both early rising and late rising have their own benefits. It depends on a person’s lifestyle, work, and body rhythm. Tabassum	: I agree. But personally, I prefer early rising because it makes me disciplined, energetic, and more productive. Faria		: And I like late rising sometimes because it gives me creativity and proper rest. Everyone can choose what suits them best. Tabassum	: Absolutely! Balance is important, but waking up early definitely gives an edge in health and success. Faria		: True! Early or late, the key is to manage time wisely and stay healthy.

Everyone can choose what suits them best. Tabassum	: Absolutely! Balance is important, but waking up early definitely gives an edge in health and success. Faria		: True! Early or late, the key is to manage time wisely and stay healthy.

text: 31. A Dialogue on Physical Exercise (Between Tabassum and Hira) Tabassum	: Hello Hira! You look very energetic today. Where are you coming from? Hira	: Hello Tabassum! I have just returned from the playground. I was doing some physical exercises with my friends. Tabassum	: That’s great! I have heard that physical exercise is very important for our health, but I don’t do it regularly. Hira		: You really should start doing it. Physical exercise keeps our body fit and active. It also refreshes our mind. Tabassum	: But I often feel lazy in the morning. That is why I cannot wake up early to exercise. Hira		: At first it may feel difficult, but once you make it a habit, it becomes easy. Even a simple morning walk or some stretching exercises can be very helpful. Tabassum	: What kinds of exercises do you usually do? Hira	: I usually start with jogging for a few minutes. Then I do some free-hand exercises like stretching, bending, and breathing exercises. Sometimes I also play badminton or run in the field. Tabassum	: That sounds interesting. Does it really help you stay healthy? Hira	: Of course. Physical exercise improves blood circulation and strengthens our muscles and bones. It also helps digestion and keeps away many diseases. Tabassum	: I see. Nowadays many students spend most of their time using mobile phones and computers. They hardly do any physical activities. Hira	: You are right. That is why many young people feel tired and weak. Without physical exercise, the body becomes inactive and unhealthy. Tabassum	: How long should we exercise every day? Hira: Doctors usually suggest at least thirty minutes of exercise daily. It can be walking, cycling, playing games, or any other physical activity. Tabassum	: I think I should start exercising from tomorrow morning. Hira	: 	That’s a very good decision. If you want, you can join me and my friends in	 the playground. Tabassum	: That would be wonderful. Exercising with friends will surely make it more enjoyable. Hira	: Exactly. Physical exercise not only improves our health but also makes us cheerful and confident. Tabassum	: Thank you, Hira. Your advice has really inspired me. Hira	: You are welcome, Tabassum. Remember, a healthy body is the key to a happy life.

text: 32. A Dialogue on the Importance of Learning English Characters: Rafi and Nabila Rafi	: Hello Nabila! You seem very busy with your English book. What are you studying? Nabila	: Hi Rafi! Yes, I am practicing English. Our teacher said that learning English is very important for our future. Rafi	: I know English is useful, but sometimes I feel it is difficult. Why do you think it is so important? Nabila	: There are many reasons. First of all, English is an international language. People from different countries use English to communicate with each other. Rafi		: That is true. When people from different nations meet, they usually speak in English. Nabila		: Exactly. If we learn English well, we can communicate with people all over the world. It helps us understand different cultures and ideas. Rafi	: I have also heard that most books on science, technology, and higher education are written in English. Nabila	: Yes, that is another important reason. If we know English, we can read many valuable books and gather knowledge easily. Rafi	: That means English helps us in education as well. Nabila	: Certainly. Many universities around the world use English as the medium of instruction. Without English, it becomes difficult to study abroad or even access online courses. Rafi	: I see. English must also be important for jobs. Nabila	: Of course. In many professions such as business, medicine, engineering, and information technology, English is widely used. A person who knows English well often gets better job opportunities. Rafi	: That makes sense. Even in our country many offices prefer employees who can speak English fluently. Nabila	: Yes. Moreover, English is also the main language of the internet. Most websites, research articles, and international news are in English. Rafi: 	So if we know English, we can learn many things from the internet and stay updated with the world. Nabila	: Absolutely. Besides, learning English improves our confidence. When we can speak English properly, we can express our ideas clearly in front of others. Rafi	: You are right. Now I realize that learning English is not only for passing exams but also for building our future. Nabila	: Exactly, Rafi. If we practice regularly, English will become easier for us. Rafi		: Thank you, Nabila. From today I will try to practice English more seriously. Nabila	: That’s great! Remember, the more we practice, the better we learn.

text: 33. A Dialogue About Opening a Bank Account Rahim			: Good morning, sir. I would like to open a bank account. Could you please tell me the procedure? Bank Officer	: Good morning. Certainly, I will help you. First of all, may I know what type of account you want to open? Rahim			: I am a student, so I think a savings account will be suitable for me. Bank Officer	: Yes, a savings account is a good choice for students. It allows you to deposit money safely and earn a small amount of interest. Rahim			: That sounds good. What documents do I need to open the account? Bank Officer	: You will need a photocopy of your national ID card or birth certificate, two passport-size photographs, and an introducer who already has an account in our bank. Rahim:			 I see. I have brought my birth certificate and photographs with me.			 My uncle also has an account here. Can he be my introducer? Bank Officer	: Of course. If your uncle signs the introduction section of the form, that will be enough. Rahim		: Thank you. Is there any minimum amount required to open the account? Bank Officer	: Yes, you have to deposit at least 500 taka as the initial amount for a savings account. Rahim		: That is quite reasonable. After opening the account, will I get a debit card? Bank Officer	: Yes, you can apply for a debit card. It will allow you to withdraw money from ATM booths and make payments easily. Rahim			: That will be very helpful. Will I also receive a cheque book? Bank Officer	: Yes, we will provide you with a cheque book after the account is activated. Rahim			: How long will it take to open the account? Bank Officer	: If you fill up the form properly and submit the required documents, the account can be opened within one or two working days. Rahim		: That is very convenient. Please give me the form. I will fill it out right now. Bank Officer	: Here is the form. Fill it up carefully, and if you face any difficulty, feel free to ask me. Rahim			: Thank you very much for your kind help. Bank Officer	: You are welcome. We are always happy to assist our customers.

I study in Class X and my roll number is 15. I live in a village which is quite far from my school. Every day I have to travel a long distance to attend my classes. As a result, I often face many difficulties on my way to school. Sometimes I arrive late and cannot concentrate properly on my studies because of the tiring journey. Moreover, it is very difficult for me to attend extra classes, group study, and other academic activities after school hours due to the long distance from my residence. If I get a seat in the school hostel, I will be able to live in a peaceful and disciplined environment which will help me concentrate more on my studies. I will also be able to follow the rules and regulations of the hostel strictly and maintain good conduct. My parents are also very eager for me to stay in the hostel so that I can continue my studies in a better way under proper supervision. Therefore, I earnestly request you to kindly grant me a seat in the school hostel. I shall remain ever grateful to you for your kindness. Yours obediently, Anika Class: X Roll: 15

text: 2. Prayer for a full-free studentship. 10th March 2025 The Principal Banani Bidyaniketan School and college Banani Model Town Banani Dhaka-1213 Subject: Prayer for a full-free studentship. Dear sir With due respect, I would like to draw your kind attention to an important matter of mine which I want to resolve with your kind assistance to continue my study. My father is a poor farmer from a remote area. Carrying the expenses of a six-member family including our four siblings is very difficult for him. Besides, along with me, my three siblings are studying in different public universities of the country. Along with other expenses of the family, the educational expenses of my siblings are about forty thousand taka. But my father's income is only thirty thousand taka only. In such a situation, if my educational expenses are added to those expenses, it will be impossible for my father to spend that amount of money. Therefore, I hope, in view of the above circumstances, You would kindly grant me a scholarship of tk.3000 per month from the poor fund of the institution and thus enable me to continue my study. Sincerely yours Amina Class viii, section D

text: 5. prayer for permission to go on a study tour. 10th March 2025 The Principal Banani Bidyaniketan School and college Banani Model Town Banani Dhaka-1213 Subject: prayer for permission to go on a study tour. Dear sir, With due respect, we, the students of your school would like to draw your gracious attention to the fact that we are willing to go on a study tour on 2nd March 2023 to Sonargaon, the ancient capital city of Bengal. We feel that an excursion is part and parcel of study. Mere bookish knowledge does not satisfy our thirst for knowledge, if we do not see things with our own eyes. Such a visit to Sonargaon and Panam Nagar will give us a clear idea about our glorious past and its historical evidences. Many people still visit the historic city of Sonargaon and Panam Nagar which have immense beauty and archaeological relics. The Folklore Museum of Sonargaon houses a variety of artifacts from all over Bangladesh, representing the culture and tradition of indigenous group of people. So this tour will undoubtedly widen our knowledge of history and tradition. It is noted that our teachers have agreed to guide us during the tour and the total costs will be borne by the respective participants. So we need only your permission and hoping you to come with us. Therefore, we pray and hope that your generosity would kindly give us permission and take the necessary arrangements to make the tour a grand success and oblige us hereby in the days to come. sincerely yours, On behalf of the students of BBNSC. Asif

An application to the mayor for taking steps towards the growth of Aedes mosquitoes in your locality. 26 August 2025 To The Mayor Dhaka North City Corporation Gulshan,Dhaka-1213 Subject: Request for immediate action to control the growth of Aedes mosquitoes in our locality. Sir, With due respect, I would like to bring to your kind attention a matter of grave concern that demands urgent attention. Our locality is currently facing a severe outbreak of Aedes mosquitoes, which has significantly increased the risk of dengue and other mosquito-borne diseases. The unchecked growth of these mosquitoes is largely due to the accumulation of stagnant water in drains, roadside ditches, abandoned containers, and construction sites. The situation has deteriorated to such an extent that daily life has become difficult. Children and elderly people are particularly vulnerable, and several residents are already suffering from dengue-related symptoms. Unless immediate and effective steps are taken, the situation may turn into a severe public health crisis. I, therefore, earnestly request you to take proper measures such as regular cleaning of drains, spraying of mosquito repellents, awareness campaigns, and strict monitoring of mosquito breeding grounds to control the growth of Aedes mosquitoes in our area. I shall remain ever grateful for your kind cooperation. Yours sincerely, [Your Name] [Your Address] [Date] [Contact Number]

text: 15. Letter to the Editor To The Editor The Daily Star 32/1Karwan Bazer,Dhaka Subject: Insufficient water supply in Kuril Vishwa Road. Sir, Through the columns of your esteemed daily, I would like to draw the attention of the concerned authority to a serious problem that we, the residents of Kuril Vishwa Road, have been facing for a long time. We hope that by publishing this letter in your widely circulated daily, the concerned authority will take necessary action as soon as possible. Yours faithfully, Disha On behalf of the people of Kuril Vishwa Road, Dhaka Acute Crisis of Water Supply in Kuril Vishwa Road The water supply in Kuril Vishwa Road area is extremely insufficient. Most of the time the supply remains irregular, and even when it comes, the pressure is so low that it cannot meet the needs of the residents. During the summer season, the problem becomes unbearable. Families suffer badly, students cannot concentrate on their studies, and the poor people are the worst victims. Often people have to wait for hours with pitchers and buckets in long queues for a small amount of water. Sometimes they are compelled to buy water at high prices which is beyond the capacity of many. We, therefore, earnestly request the authority of WASA to take immediate steps to ensure an adequate and regular water supply in Kuril Vishwa Road. Only then the suffering of the people will be reduced.

text: 17. Application for Increasing Library Facilities 21 August 2025 The Principal Viqarunnisa Noon School and College Dhaka 1216 Subject: Prayer for increasing library facilities. Dear Sir, With due respect, I, on behalf of the students of your school, beg to state that our school library is rich in books but not sufficient to meet the growing demand of the students. As the library is the center of knowledge and plays a vital role in improving the academic standard of the students, it is essential to increase library facilities. Supplying more updated books, reference materials, and daily newspapers will enrich our knowledge. Expanding the reading room and providing more chairs and tables will also help us a lot. Therefore, I earnestly request you to take the necessary steps to increase our library facilities so that we can make the best use of our valuable time in studies. Sincerely yours, Disha Class– 10 On behalf of the students of Viqarunnisa School and College

text: 18. Application for Sending Relief Goods to Flood-Affected People 21 August 2025 The Mayor Dhaka North City Corporation Dhaka. Subject: Prayer for sending relief goods for the flood-affected people. Dear Sir, With due respect, I, on behalf of the people of Amtoli village under Dhaka district , would like to draw your kind attention to the miserable condition of the flood-affected people. Recently, a devastating flood has swept over this region. Houses, roads, schools, and croplands are under water. Many families have become homeless and are passing their days under the open sky without food, pure drinking water, or medical treatment. Children and elderly people are suffering the most. Diseases like diarrhea and fever are spreading alarmingly. The affected people are in great distress. They are badly in need of dry food, pure drinking water, clothes, medicines, and other emergency supplies. Unless immediate steps are taken, their sufferings will increase beyond measure. In these circumstances, we earnestly request you to kindly send sufficient relief goods, medical teams, and necessary support to our flood-affected area at the earliest possible time. Your timely action will save many helpless people from starvation, disease, and untold sufferings. We hope you will take immediate and effective steps to lessen the sufferings of the distressed people. Sincerely yours, Roja On behalf of the flood-affected people Amtoli, Dhaka

text: 19. An application for sending relief goods and medical facilities to flood affected people. 25 November 2025 The Deputy Commissioner Patuakhali. Subject: Request for Relief Goods and Medical Aid for Flood-Affected People Dear sir, I am writing to draw your kind attention to the devastating floods that have recently affected our district, Patuakhali. Due to continuous heavy rainfall and overflowing rivers, many villages have been completely submerged, forcing thousands of families to leave their homes. The flood has destroyed crops, homes, roads, and essential infrastructure, leaving the people in extreme hardship. The situation is particularly alarming for vulnerable groups such as children, the elderly, pregnant women, and the sick. Many families are living in temporary shelters without proper food, drinking water, clothing, or medical care. The lack of proper sanitation and medical facilities is increasing the risk of diseases such as diarrhea, cholera, and other waterborne illnesses. Immediate relief is essential to prevent further suffering and loss of life among the affected people. In view of this urgent situation, I humbly request your esteemed office to provide relief goods and medical assistance to the flood-affected population of our district. Essential items such as food, drinking water, clothing, blankets, and medicines should be distributed as soon as possible to help the victims survive and recover from this disaster. I sincerely hope that your office will take prompt action to assist the flood-affected people of Mirzagonj, Patuakhali. Yours sincerely Asif on behalf of the people of Mirzagonj, Patuakhali
